channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: From classifying immigrants as dead to deportation: A guide to actions on Trump immigration policies
item.description: President Donald Trump's immigration agenda is playing out in numerous ways Friday, from hearings in key cases on the government's power to deport people to the start of a registry required for all those who are in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River near the New Jersey shoreline, killing the pilot and a Spanish family of five who were on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US measles cases surpass 700 with outbreaks in six states. Here's what to know
item.description: U.S. measles cases topped 700 as of Friday, capping a week in which Indiana joined five others states with active outbreaks, Texas grew by another 60 cases and a third measles-related death was made public. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jewish families channel Passover story after devastating losses in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: PASADENA, California (AP) — Aty Rotter lost her family home and her spiritual home in the ravenous fire that scorched a large swath of Los Angeles County earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge finds
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be kicked out of the U.S. as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana found Friday during a hearing over the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minecraft 'Chicken Jockey' trend hits Utah theaters, movie-goer kicked out after bringing live chicken
item.description: Videos online show crowds watching 'A Minecraft Movie' in theaters cheering wildly as part of a viral trend — but, after a recent event with streamers, air horns, and a live chicken in a Utah theater, some are saying the trend has gone too far.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Feds say agents went to LA schools to do welfare check on migrant children
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Homeland Security officers visited two Los Angeles public elementary schools this week to do a welfare check on migrant children, not for immigration enforcement, the department said Friday. The officers were denied access by both principals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida universities to deputize campus police for immigration enforcement
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The police departments of at least three public universities in Florida are seeking agreements with the federal government to carry out immigration enforcement on campus.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tufts student from Turkey details arrest, crowded detention conditions in new court filing
item.description: A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey is demanding her release after she was detained by immigration officials near her Massachusetts home, detailing how she was scared when the men grabbed her phone and feared she would be killed.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration (SSA) to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six people were killed Thursday in the latest string of aviation disasters after a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey. The crash comes at a time of heightened scrutiny of aircraft collisions and near-misses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Could Trump's tariffs slow emissions? Sure, experts say, but at great cost overall
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs have stirred widespread anxiety about a severe economic downturn -- and curiosity, for some, about how it might affect the world’s warming climate.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart employee accused of killing co-worker inside store
item.description: A Walmart employee shot and killed a co-worker inside a Georgia store and critically wounded another worker outside early Friday while the business was closed to the public, authorities said. Newton County deputies responded to the shooting at the Walmart in Covington, Georgia, at around 1:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 American women arrested at border for smuggling unaccompanied minors: CBP
item.description: Two U.S. citizens have been arrested for smuggling unaccompanied Mexican girls into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration offers no details on mistakenly deported man after court ruling
item.description: A federal judge lambasted a government lawyer who couldn’t explain what, if anything, President Donald Trump’s administration has done to arrange for the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US government moves for release of ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about the Bidens
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The U.S. government now wants a former FBI informant who fabricated a story about former President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes to be released from prison while he appeals his conviction, it was revealed Thursday in court records.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mourners pay tribute to merengue icon Rubby Pérez, who died in the Dominican roof collapse
item.description: Fans and countrymen on Thursday shared their remembrances of Dominican Republic music icon Rubby Pérez, who was among the scores of people killed this week after the roof at the Jet Set club in Santo Domingo collapsed. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Shark attack survivor presses state for alert system to keep people safe in the water
item.description: An Alabama teen who narrowly survived a fierce shark attack last year said she hopes a proposed alert system before state lawmakers can help keep others safe in the water.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rumor of monkey trade for child leads to Missouri foster mom's arrest
item.description: Officials say new child abuse charges filed against a Winfield woman who has fostered children for a decade are "just the tip of the iceberg.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Students react to nearly 50 international students' visas being revoked, records being removed across Utah
item.description: International students at institutions across Utah and around the country are having their records terminated or their visas revoked. Here's what some students said about it.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Texas lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, KRON4

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shark attack survivor presses her state for an alert system to keep people safe in the water
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama teen who narrowly survived a fierce shark attack last year said she hopes a proposed alert system before state lawmakers can help keep others safe in the water.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: If approved, the increase for a “forever” stamp and similar increases for postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect July 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Four Americans accused of Congo coup attempt now facing federal charges in Salt Lake City
item.description: Four Americans accused of participating in a failed coup attempt in the Democratic Republic of Congo will now go to trial in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: NYC lets immigration officials open an office at Rikers jail, a priority for Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams ' administration is allowing federal immigration officials to operate at Rikers Island to work on gang and drug-related criminal investigations in the city's largest lockup.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Teen seen shoving guns through border wall into Mexico
item.description: An Arizona teen allegedly was caught slipping guns and knives through the border wall south into Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: YouTuber and entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: MrBeast, whose real name is Jimmy Donaldson, called the tariffs a "nail in the coffin" for small businesses.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
